state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Ohio’s Maumee-to-Ohio River belt mixes industrial-scale grain with muck-soil vegetables near Lake Erie.
fun_facts: Ohio ranks among the top states for Swiss cheese and other specialty dairy.
Heirloom tomato festivals highlight late-summer abundance.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Indiana’s tile-drained fields support corn and soy rotation; southern valleys still produce signature melons and sweet corn.
fun_facts: Indiana tomato production underpins major processing contracts each summer.
Pumpkin patches and apple orchards drive fall agritourism statewide.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Illinois leads in soybeans and sits among top corn states—Farm Progress Show country mixes with Chicago-area vegetable producers.
fun_facts: The Morton pumpkin festival celebrates Illinois’ claim on pie-grade processing squash.
Northern apple orchards mirror Great Lakes climate patterns.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Michigan produces most of the nation’s tart cherries and leads blueberry acreage; lake effect lengthens orchard viability.
fun_facts: Traverse City calls itself the Cherry Capital of the World.
Oceana County’s asparagus reaches early national markets.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Wisconsin’s dairy complex pairs with massive cranberry marshes and seed potato certification programs.
fun_facts: Wisconsin produces more cheese than any other state.
Wood County leads national cranberry production by value.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Minnesota’s Red River Valley grows sugar beets and small grains; controlled wild rice harvests support Ojibwe economies.
fun_facts: Minnesota often leads U.S. turkey slaughter numbers.
Farmers’ market seasons concentrate into intense northern summers.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Iowa’s deep soils make it the Corn Belt’s core, yet vibrant CSAs still layer vegetables onto the landscape.
fun_facts: Iowa typically leads the nation in corn and soybean production combined.
Sweet corn at the state fair is a cultural institution.

state_profiles.agricultural_highlights: Missouri’s terraced Ozark pastures contrast with Mississippi Delta-style rice and cotton in the south.
fun_facts: St. James claims the title Table Grape Capital of Missouri for Norton wine grapes.
Kansas City and St. Louis anchor large urban eat-local movements.
